architecture_fit.note: 3-layer transformer + MLP with no ablation justifying depth (goal asks to start at 1 layer); numerically high but only on its own training set.

baseline_comparison.note: Reports the goal-provided linear baseline lift, but adds no architectural strawman (no-attention/no-MLP) of its own.

faithfulness.note: No ablation or patching; trains and evaluates on the identical generate(seed=0) batch, so metrics show memorization, and the README never discloses or addresses this.

operating_range.note: Sweeps carries 0-3 (the goal axis) but only on training data; no held-out seed, no digit-width/scale generalization, non-monotonic carry curve betrays overfitting.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: N/A — fully trained model, no hand-set circuit attempted.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is interactive with operand inputs, carry presets, per-slice metrics and benchmark panel — legible, though it probes a model that memorized the eval set.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies the interactive probe and carry presets, but it's more demo than analytical chart and doesn't tie axes/baseline to the carry-robustness claim.

notes: A reasonable base_model-style transformer with a clean interactive app and strong-looking carry_robustness (0.97), but the headline result is undermined because main.py trains on generate(seed=0) and evaluate tests on the same batch — train equals test, so the numbers measure memorization rather than learned carry propagation. The non-monotonic per-carry curve (carries=1/2 worse than carries=3) is a memorization tell, and there is no ablation, held-out set, or causal evidence. The architecture also uses 3 layers with no justification against the goal's minimal-layer expectation.
